postData.contentHtml:
The inaugural 2026 Nations Championship saw a thrilling start for New Zealand's All Blacks as they secured a commanding 47-17 win against Italy. The highly anticipated rugby clash took place on July 11, 2026, at HNRY Stadium in New Zealand, setting an exciting tone for the new global tournament.

A standout moment of the match was Will Jordan's exceptional performance, as he scored a hat-trick to become the All Blacks' all-time leading try-scorer – a truly historic achievement in New Zealand rugby. This significant victory followed the All Blacks' narrow triumph over France in their previous outing, while Italy, unfortunately, entered the game on the back of a loss to Japan.

Fans across the globe were keen to catch the action. For those in the UK, the match was freely available to stream on ITVX. International viewers, however, navigated geo-restrictions by utilizing Virtual Private Networks (VPNs) like ExpressVPN or Proton VPN, connecting to a UK server to access the live broadcast. This highlights the growing trend of using technology to ensure access to major sports events worldwide.

Introducing a new format for rugby, the 2026 Nations Championship brings together top teams from both the northern and southern hemispheres, promising an exhilarating series of contests. Social media buzz around the New Zealand vs. Italy match reflected a generally positive sentiment, with fans engaging in lively discussions about team strategies and individual player brilliance.
postData.title: All Blacks Dominate Italy in Nations Championship Opener, Will Jordan Makes History
postData.summary: New Zealand's All Blacks kicked off their 2026 Nations Championship campaign with a decisive 47-17 victory over Italy, marked by a record-breaking performance from Will Jordan. Discover the match highlights and how fans tuned in.
